name: Liver Metastasis Stratification
what_it_does: Stratifies BreAK CRC-001 patients by liver metastasis status (present/absent) and computes the expected IO benefit differential
why_it_matters: CO.26 Pint=0.02 — liver metastasis is the single most important IO stratification variable in MSS CRC. Without this stratification, the trial cannot detect IO benefit in NLM patients.
expected_output: Stratified enrollment table; expected PFS HR by liver metastasis status; recommendation for NLM enrichment

name: pTMB ≥28 Enrichment
what_it_does: Identifies patients with pTMB ≥28 (GuardantOMNI) and computes the expected IO benefit in this subgroup
why_it_matters: CO.26 pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 — the strongest IO enrichment signal in MSS CRC from randomized data
expected_output: pTMB distribution in enrolled patients; pTMB ≥28 subgroup size; expected OS HR in pTMB ≥28 subgroup

name: KRAS Mutation Subtype Ranking
what_it_does: Ranks KRAS mutation subtypes (G12C, G12D, G12V, G13D) by expected vaccine antigen immunogenicity and IO permissiveness
why_it_matters: KRAS mutations are present in ~40% of MSS mCRC. Different KRAS subtypes have different neoantigen profiles and different IO permissiveness. KRAS G12D/G12V are the most common in CRC.
expected_output: KRAS subtype distribution; expected IO permissiveness by subtype; recommendation for KRAS-stratified analysis

name: Baseline ctDNA / Disease Burden
what_it_does: Measures baseline ctDNA level as a proxy for disease burden and IO permissiveness
why_it_matters: GRANITE GO-010: ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50 (directional benefit). Low disease burden correlates with better IO response.
expected_output: ctDNA distribution; ctDNA-low subgroup definition; expected IO benefit by ctDNA level

name: Immunoscore-IC / CD8+ Baseline
what_it_does: Measures Immunoscore-IC (CD8+ TIL density at tumor center and invasive margin) at baseline
why_it_matters: AtezoTRIBE: Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 (exploratory). Immune-inflamed TME is required for IO benefit.
expected_output: Immunoscore-IC distribution; immune-inflamed vs excluded vs desert classification; expected IO benefit by TME phenotype

name: CPI Class Ranking
what_it_does: Ranks checkpoint inhibitor classes (PD-1, PD-L1, CTLA-4, LAG-3, TIM-3) by expected synergy with STC-1010
why_it_matters: STC-1010 primes T cells — checkpoint inhibitors release the brakes. The optimal CPI class depends on the dominant checkpoint in the MSS CRC TME.
required_inputs: Checkpoint expression data (PD-L1, LAG-3, TIM-3 IHC)
expected_output: CPI class ranking by expected synergy; recommendation for CPI partner
blocker: OQ-01 — open question: which CPI class is optimal for STC-1010 combination

name: Antigen Coverage Ranking
what_it_does: Ranks STC-1010 antigens (CEACAM5, CDK1, EPHA2) by expected immunogenicity and tumor coverage in MSS mCRC
why_it_matters: Antigen coverage determines the breadth of the vaccine-induced immune response. Higher coverage = more tumor cells killed.
required_inputs: Antigen expression data from patient biopsies
HLA typing for antigen presentation prediction
expected_output: Antigen coverage ranking; expected immunogenicity by antigen; recommendation for antigen prioritization
blocker: B-001 — requires patient biopsy antigen expression data not yet available

name: Bevacizumab Arm Assignment
what_it_does: Determines optimal bevacizumab arm assignment based on VEGF pathway activity and liver metastasis status
why_it_matters: Bevacizumab normalizes tumor vasculature, potentially improving T cell trafficking to the tumor. The benefit may be greater in patients with high VEGF activity.
expected_output: Bevacizumab arm assignment recommendation; expected benefit by VEGF activity
blocker: OQ-02 — open question: does bevacizumab improve STC-1010 efficacy?

name: Concurrent Vaccine + Chemo Sequencing Risk
what_it_does: Assesses the risk of concurrent vaccine administration with mFOLFOX6 chemotherapy
why_it_matters: Chemotherapy can suppress the immune response needed for vaccine efficacy. The optimal sequencing (vaccine before, during, or after chemo) is not established.
expected_output: Sequencing risk assessment; recommendation for optimal vaccine-chemo sequencing

name: MSS vs. MSI-H Arm Stratification
what_it_does: Confirms MSS/pMMR status for all enrolled patients and stratifies by MMR gene variant
why_it_matters: BreAK CRC-001 is designed for MSS/pMMR patients. MSI-H patients respond to checkpoint inhibitors alone — they should not be enrolled in a vaccine trial. Contamination with MSI-H patients would inflate the IO response rate.
expected_output: MSS/MSI-H classification; MMR gene variant distribution; enrollment eligibility confirmation

name: Prior IO Exposure Stratification
what_it_does: Stratifies patients by prior IO exposure (checkpoint inhibitor naive vs. prior CPI)
why_it_matters: Prior CPI exposure may create acquired resistance mechanisms (PD-L1 upregulation, T cell exhaustion) that reduce vaccine efficacy.
expected_output: Prior IO exposure distribution; expected vaccine efficacy by prior IO status

name: BRAF WT Eligibility Confirmation
what_it_does: Confirms BRAF wild-type status for all enrolled patients
why_it_matters: SC-001 ACTIVE CONFLICT: CT26 (one of the three STC-1010 cell lines) carries BRAF V600E. BreAK CRC-001 Exclusion Criterion #7 requires BRAF wild-type patients. This scientific inconsistency must be resolved.
expected_output: BRAF WT confirmation; BRAF V600E exclusion rate
blocker: SC-001 ACTIVE CONFLICT — must be resolved before enrollment

name: QUILT-2.004 Design Family Concordance
what_it_does: Validates BreAK CRC-001 design against QUILT-2.004 (the closest design precedent) and identifies design improvements
why_it_matters: QUILT-2.004 (vaccine + IO + mFOLFOX6 + bev) failed with HR 1.061 (p=0.91). BreAK CRC-001 must demonstrate how it addresses the QUILT-2.004 failure modes.
expected_output: Design concordance table; QUILT-2.004 failure mode analysis; BreAK CRC-001 design improvements
blocker: OQ-01 — open question: does BreAK CRC-001 address QUILT-2.004 failure modes?

name: Preliminary STC-1010 Clinical Signal
what_it_does: Analyzes preliminary clinical data from STC-1010 (AACR 2026 CT051) to identify early efficacy signals
why_it_matters: AACR 2026 CT051 (press release only) — no quantitative values available. Preliminary data would inform the biomarker strategy.
expected_output: Preliminary efficacy signal; biomarker correlates; recommendation for Phase II design
blocker: B-003 — AACR 2026 CT051 data is press-release only; no quantitative values available

name: Stromal Barrier / Decorin / TGF-β
what_it_does: Measures stromal barrier markers (TGF-β, decorin, CAF density) at baseline to identify immune-excluded patients
why_it_matters: Immune-excluded patients have T cells at the tumor margin but cannot penetrate the stromal barrier. TGF-β inhibition could convert excluded to inflamed — but only if the barrier is identified first.
expected_output: Stromal barrier score; immune-excluded vs inflamed classification; recommendation for TGF-β inhibition combination

name: GBM Responder Analysis — GLASS/TCGA/CGGA
what_it_does: Defines OS ≥ 18mo responders across 3 GBM cohorts (n=1,003); generates KM curves and ZEB1/SOX2 molecular signal validation in GLASS pairs.
why_it_matters: Establishes cross-cohort consistency of the responder signal (2.6×–3.9× survival ratio) and validates ZEB1/SOX2 as molecular correlates of non-response in GBM.

name: CRC PDS IPD Responder Analysis — PACCE/PRIME/PEAK/N0147
what_it_does: Defines PFS ≥ 6mo (mCRC) and DFS ≥ 3yr (adjuvant) responders across 4 PDS CRC trials (n=5,691); generates KM curves.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates 3.3×–4.3× survival ratios in real PDS IPD, validating the responder framework in CRC with the strongest data grade.

name: CRC Literature Acquisition — MOSAIC/VELOUR/HORIZON III
what_it_does: Extracts published summary statistics and generates pseudo-IPD (parametric exponential) for 3 PDS DESCRIPTOR_ONLY CRC trials (n=4,894).
why_it_matters: Extends the responder evidence base to DESCRIPTOR_ONLY caslibs; HORIZON III identity correction (cediranib, not XELOX) prevents misuse of caslib 78 as SoC reference.
critical_flags: HORIZON III = cediranib (VEGFR-TKI) vs bevacizumab — NEGATIVE trial. Caslib 78 must NOT be used as SoC reference.
Pseudo-IPD grade: IPD_LITERATURE_PSEUDO — not for regulatory use

name: Cross-Cohort Forest Plot — 10 Cohorts, 11,588 Patients
what_it_does: Generates forest plot of responder vs non-responder survival ratios across all 10 cohorts (3 GBM + 7 CRC). v2 adds MOSAIC, VELOUR, HORIZON III.
why_it_matters: Single figure demonstrating cross-disease, cross-data-grade consistency of the responder signal. HORIZON III serves as negative control (effect < 1.0).
